title: Facilities, Racks, Cabling, Power, and Environment · Incident Defense
summary: Plan an MDF, IDFs, racks, patching, power, cooling, security, and maintenance access for a new site. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a facilities-ready network installation package. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints.
Document patch panels, fiber distribution panels, labels, polarity, ports, pathways, and change ownership.
Align port-side intake or exhaust, hot and cold airflow, lockable access, and maintenance reach.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Environment Fire applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.
